items: Funding valuation of the Howard and Sons defined benefit plan
Prepared for the Director of Operations and the plan's trustees
Engagement commenced {{fact:f:E-2016-001.start}}
Presented by Jennifer Vasquez, Consulting Actuary

blocks.text: What We Were Asked To Do

items: Measure plan liabilities on the funding basis at the valuation date
Compare liabilities against trust assets and develop the recommended contribution
Explain the movement in funded position since the prior valuation
Deliver the exhibit workbook and the auditor disclosure schedules
Work performed under the Actuarial Standards of Practice; opinion signed by Robert Lawrence
Census extract received and reconciled to the prior population by David Sherman
Plan provisions summary drafted from the plan document and amendments by Jeffrey Ochoa
Two data items still open: beneficiary dates of birth on a subset of inactives, and one amendment
Assumption review underway; nothing recommended yet
Valuation runs begin once the open items close

blocks.text: Data and What We Rely On

items: We rely on the census, the trust statements, and the plan document as furnished by Elizabeth Horne and her staff
We review for consistency and reconcile against the prior valuation; we do not audit
Where an item is unavailable we assume, and we disclose both the assumption and the fact of it
Data quality drives the schedule more than anything else on our side
Measurement is what the data show; judgment is what we assume about the future
Mortality is the live question this cycle and we expect it to move the liability
We will show the effect of any assumption change separately, so you can see it
We recommend; the board adopts
No number leaves this engagement before it is reviewed and signed
Close the two open data items with your recordkeeper
Complete the provisions check and the assumption review
Run the valuation and build the exhibits
Draft results to Howard and Sons for review before anything goes to the board
Questions to Jennifer Vasquez; data questions direct to David Sherman
